node-1.title: Fire Detected
node-1.situation: Smoke alarms are triggered on the second floor of the building. Maintenance reports smoke near the storage area.
node-1.question: What is your first action?

options.label: A
options.text: Immediately evacuate the building and call emergency services.

options.label: B
options.text: Investigate the source of smoke before taking action.

options.label: C
options.text: Ignore the alarm and assume it is a false positive.

node-2.title: Evacuation Initiated
node-2.situation: The building evacuation is underway. Emergency services have been notified. Some employees are still inside the building.
node-2.question: How do you proceed?

options.label: A
options.text: Conduct a headcount at the assembly point to identify anyone missing.

options.label: B
options.text: Re-enter the building to search for remaining employees.

node-3.title: Smoke Source Found
node-3.situation: You find a small fire in the storage room. It is growing rapidly and blocking the main exit.
node-3.question: What do you do?

options.label: A
options.text: Use the fire extinguisher and attempt to control the fire.

options.label: B
options.text: Immediately leave and activate the fire alarm for full evacuation.

node-4.title: Incident Resolved
node-4.situation: Emergency services have arrived and the situation is under control. All employees are safe at the assembly point.
node-4.question: What is your final step?

options.label: A
options.text: Brief emergency responders and provide a full incident report.

node-5.title: Scenario Complete
node-5.situation: The incident has been successfully managed. Emergency services have cleared the scene. You can now re-enter the building safely.
node-5.question: Scenario complete. Well done!
